description: This video discusses the Bellman-Held-Karp algorithm for solving the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) using dynamic programming. It highlights the challenges of NP-hard problems and the trade-off between correctness and speed. The video also introduces Stirling's approximation to illustrate the growth of factorial functions compared to exponential functions.
topic_summary: In this section, the focus is on designing exact algorithms for NP-hard problems, specifically the TSP. The Bellman-Held-Karp algorithm is presented as a more efficient alternative to naive exhaustive search, with an expected running time of O(2^n) compared to O(n!). The video also explains the implications of Stirling's approximation on algorithm performance.
